column1: Baseball is one of the many bat-and-ball games. It has enjoyed great_____________in the USA for more than 150 years. (POPULAR)
Baseball is almost the same as the British game rounders, although the two games have slightly____________rules. (DIFFER)
No-one knows for sure where baseball or rounders were____________played. (ORIGINAL)
Cricket also shares several_______________with both baseball and rounders. (SIMILAR)
One___________of cricket, at least for those learning the game, is that it has some strange rules and a complicated scoring system. (ADVANTAGE)
Before the Second World War, "youth culture" didn't exist. (TWENTY)
This was the first_______________of young people with money to spend on music, clothes and going out. (GENERATE)
It was a time when everyone was_____________dressed and young people filled clubs, cinemas and music venues. (FABULOUS)
Many parents thought young people had______________attitudes, and objected to the music they listened to. (SHOCK)
They also often complained about their children's______________because of the modern clothes they wore and their strange haircuts. (APPEAR)
Nowadays, youth culture is appreciated for its_____________and how much it contributes to culture in general. (CREATIVE)
